prompt:
Last week someone tidied the darkroom-tracker crate — moved entry.rs into a new src/log/ directory, split the chemistry helpers out — and pushed without building. Now it's mine and cargo greets me with this:

  error[E0432]: unresolved import `super::chem`
   --> src/log/entry.rs:1:12  — could not find `chem` in `super`
  error[E0432]: unresolved import `crate::entry`
   --> src/report.rs:4:12
  error[E0432]: unresolved import `crate::log::FilmStock`
   --> src/chem.rs:4:9  — no `FilmStock` in `log`
   note: struct `crate::log::entry::FilmStock` exists but is inaccessible
  error[E0603]: function `dilution` is private
   --> src/report.rs:3:19
  error: could not compile `rs-darkroom` (lib) due to 4 previous errors

The FilmStock one baffles me most: chem re-exports it from log, and log re-exports it from chem, and somehow neither is the one that has it. Heads up that the list above grew as I chipped at it — fixing a path would just uncover a new privacy complaint somewhere else, so don't trust that these four are the whole story.

Nothing about the printed reports may change: tests/darkroom.rs pins the contact sheet text, the dilution labels, the frame cap, and that FilmStock stays reachable under BOTH module paths (the shopping-list code uses the chem one). `cargo test --offline` green; tests/, Cargo.toml and .gitignore are off limits. And keep the tidy-up's module layout — entry belongs to log now; don't just move files back.
notes: Module-system triage after a file move: stale `super::` path in log/entry.rs (was correct at crate root), stale `crate::entry` path in report.rs, missing pub on 3 items (mod entry in log/mod.rs; fn dilution in chem.rs -> E0603; Entry::sheet_frames -> E0624), and a circular re-export — chem.rs `pub use crate::log::FilmStock` + log/mod.rs `pub use crate::chem::FilmStock` while the struct actually lives in log/entry.rs; resolved by re-pointing log's re-export at self::entry (chem's shim then resolves through it, which the both-paths test pins). Chain-reveals, documented per 5C law and flagged in the prompt: rustc 1.96.0 shows 4 errors at baseline; the mod-entry privacy (E0603) and sheet_frames (E0624) surface only after the resolution errors are fixed.
